facts.text: Lantana got its name from the wild lantana plants that grew in abundance, as chosen by postmaster Lyman for the town’s first post office.
After Congress passed the Armed Occupation Act of 1842, the Lyman family settled in 1888, opened store and post office, and incorporated July 20, 1921 with 22 voters.
In the early 1900s, oyster gathering and marketing became Lantana’s leading industry.
When Interstate 95 was completed through Lantana in the mid‑1970s, commercial development surged.
From 1974 to 1988, Lantana hosted the world’s largest decorated Christmas tree, shipped each year by rail to the National Enquirer grounds.
Lantana enjoys a tropical rainforest climate with a mild, dry winter from November to April and hot, wet summers that bring frequent brief thunderstorms.
